title: parahebe carstensensis
intro: Parahebe carstensensis, a species of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae, is a native New Zealand herb also known as the "Carstensens Parahebe" or "Tahina". This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Parahebe carstensensis include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and alleviate congestion, making it a valuable remedy for colds and flu. Herbal preparations of Parahebe carstensensis can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea or by using the plant's essential oils in a topical application.
